name: South Sudan
experiences: White Nile sunset cruise below Juba Bridge, paired with Konyo Konyo Market and the John Garang memorial.
Nimule National Park wildlife drives for elephants, hippos, buffalo, baboons, and dramatic granite rapids on the Nile.
Boma National Park dry-season migration country, where white-eared kob, tiang, and Mongalla gazelle move across one of Africa's least-visited savannahs.
Jebel Kujur viewpoints and low-key city orientation in Juba before committing to long overland park sectors.
Remote camp-based travel using boats, convoy-style 4x4s, and charter-style aviation rather than casual independent hopping.
avoid: Strong safety advisory: South Sudan is the world's youngest nation and remains affected by active conflict, armed checkpoints, and abrupt security changes; only attempt a fully arranged trip with current local clearance, daylight-only movements, and embassy-level contingency planning.
A high-clearance 4x4 with a vetted driver is essential for Nimule and effectively mandatory for Boma logistics; roads deteriorate fast outside the Dec-Mar dry season and self-drive is a poor idea.

Do not plan your trip around published domestic schedules from South Supreme Airlines: service is irregular at best, charters are often the only workable air option, and cash, fuel, and medical support thin out quickly once you leave Juba.
stopoverNote: Use Juba as the only serious logistics base for cash, permits, SIMs, and security checks, then treat Nimule as a long day or one-night 4x4 leg and Boma as a pre-arranged expedition; if anyone mentions South Supreme Airlines, verify on the ground and still keep a Juba buffer night because charters and road convoys remain the dependable fallback.

cities.name: Juba
cities.notes: Capital gateway for White Nile views, hotel compounds, money exchange, and the only realistic starting point for both Nimule road trips and Boma expedition planning.

cities.name: Nimule
cities.notes: Uganda-border park town where Nimule National Park, Nile rapids, and elephant country are the main draw, usually reached by a long escorted drive from Juba.

cities.name: Boma National Park
cities.notes: Extremely remote migration landscape in the Greater Pibor area, best tackled as a camp-based safari using African Parks-linked logistics, local guides, and either a hard overland convoy or ad-hoc charter access.

itinerary.sim: MTN South Sudan and Zain South Sudan are the practical Juba SIM choices; expect roughly ₹650–₹1,950 for a starter top-up, then assume weak or no useful data in Nimule's deeper park zones and across Boma, so keep offline maps and WhatsApp checkpoints downloaded before leaving the capital.

days.theme: Settle into the capital, sort practical logistics, and use the White Nile for the least stressful first look at South Sudan.

activities.name: Hotel-arranged airport transfer from Juba International Airport
John Garang Mausoleum and independence-quarter stop
Konyo Konyo Market taxi loop with a trusted local driver
Afex White Nile / Juba Bridge cruise (60–90 minutes)

days.theme: Use a buffer day for security checks, city viewpoints, and locking the transport that makes the rest of the trip possible.

activities.name: Jebel Kujur hill drive or short guided hike for city views
All Saints Cathedral and central Juba taxi hops
4x4 hire confirmation for Nimule or Boma sectors
Domestic-air desk check for South Supreme Airlines or charter alternatives

days.theme: A dry-season park day for Nile scenery, elephant country, and the easiest wildlife access South Sudan currently offers.

activities.name: Private escorted 4x4 from Juba to Nimule and park circuit
Nimule National Park foreign visitor entry
Ranger-guided wildlife drive for elephants, buffalo, hippos, and baboons
White Nile / rapid-side boat or river-view stop near Nimule

days.theme: Keep a second day only if the border road is slow or you want more time for viewpoints and a safer daylight return to Juba.

activities.name: Second park-entry day or local ranger extension
Granite-rapid photography stops and short guided walks
Return 4x4 from Nimule to Juba

days.theme: This is expedition travel: reach camp, get briefed by local guides, and use the first afternoon to read the scale of the migration plains.

activities.name: Convoy-style 4x4 transfer from Juba via the Greater Pibor corridor or equivalent expedition logistics
Boma National Park foreign visitor entry
Local migration guide and vehicle game-viewing fee
Park-side sundowner and briefing at camp

days.theme: Use a full dry-season day to look for the white-eared kob, tiang, and Mongalla gazelle movement that makes Boma globally significant.

activities.name: Full-day migration tracking drive across Boma plains
Short guided walk around camp or nearby village zone where security conditions allow
Camp meals and logistics contribution
Boat or wetland-edge access where seasonal channels are navigable

days.theme: Keep a final day only if you need more wildlife time or weather and security have already stretched the approach leg.

activities.name: Charter-style extraction or long 4x4 return toward Juba
Second game drive focusing on open-grassland antelope movement

connections.method: Use a vetted 4x4 and driver for the 5-6 hour dry-season run; public transport exists in theory but is not the right safety or comfort choice for a travel-planner itinerary.

Return through Juba, then continue only on a pre-arranged expedition plan: either a hard overland convoy via Greater Pibor or an ad-hoc charter because South Supreme Airlines schedules are too unreliable to anchor a connection.
Exit with the same operator you entered with; long 4x4 convoys are more common than dependable domestic seats, so leave a capital buffer night before any international flight.

extras: December to March is the real travel window: roads dry out, Nimule game viewing is easier, and Boma's migration plains are far more practical than in the long rains.
Treat South Sudan as a high-risk destination rather than a casual add-on: use trusted fixers, keep movements daylight-only, and expect same-week itinerary changes if security deteriorates.
Juba is the only place where hotel choice, reliable meals, cash, and SIM setup are easy; once you leave it, infrastructure drops sharply and camp logistics dominate the budget.
If a domestic air option is offered under the South Supreme Airlines name, confirm it locally and still keep charter and 4x4 backup plans because cancellations or route changes are common.
Carry fresh USD cash, malaria protection, yellow-fever proof, and offline maps; medical evacuation capability matters much more here than in a typical East Africa circuit.
